p2.nickname: With all your heart (the long one)

p2v13.english: If you really listen — love God and live it, with all your heart and all your soul —

p2v14.english: then the rain comes when your land needs it — autumn rain, spring rain — and you gather your grain, your wine, your oil.

p2v15.english: Grass in your fields for your animals — and you’ll eat, and you’ll be full.

p2v16.english: But watch yourselves — hearts drift, and it’s easy to end up serving other things.

p2v17.english: Then everything dries up — the sky closes, no rain, nothing grows — and the good land slips away.

p2v18.english: So keep these words on your heart and soul; tie them on your hand, let them sit between your eyes.

p2v19.english: Teach them to your kids — at home, on the road, lying down, getting up. Sound familiar?

p2v20.english: Write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates.

p2v21.english: So that you and your kids live long on the promised land — days as long as the sky is over the earth.

meaningCard.title: Actions ripple
meaningCard.body: This paragraph was written for farmers, so it talks in rain: listen well and the rain comes, drift away and the sky dries up. You don’t have to read it as a weather forecast. Read it as the way the world actually works — what you do ripples out into the world you live in. Listening, the Torah says, is something the land itself can feel.

facts.body: Wait — at home, on the road, lying down, getting up… you’ve read this before. The Torah repeats it on purpose: first it told YOU to keep these words close. Now it tells the whole community. Same words, bigger circle.

p3v38.english: Tell the Israelites: put fringes on the corners of your clothes, in every generation — with one thread of sky-blue.

p3v39.english: You’ll see the fringe and remember what matters — and do it, instead of just chasing whatever your heart and eyes run after.

p3v40.english: Remember, do, and be holy — set apart for your God.

p3v41.english: I am the Eternal your God, who brought you out of Egypt to be your God. I am the Eternal your God.

meaningCard.title: Freedom is the punchline
meaningCard.body: Fringes on your clothes, a thread of sky-blue — little physical reminders, because everyone’s heart and eyes wander. And then the last line lands: I brought you out of Egypt. The whole Shema ends on freedom. You remember, you notice, you choose — because you’re free to. That’s the note it goes out on, and it’s yours now too.

facts.body: T’CHEI-let is a sky-blue dye that came from one specific sea snail. The recipe was lost for more than a thousand years — nobody could make the blue thread — and only recently did researchers track the snail down again. Some tzitzit today carry that exact blue once more.
